title: शिव पञ्चाक्षर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीशिवपञ्चाक्षरस्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय शान्ताय कारणत्रयहेतवे।
निवेदयामि चात्मानं त्वं गतिः परमेश्वर॥१॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय शुद्धाय ब्रह्मरूपधराय च।
सच्चिदानन्दरूपाय कृष्णपिङ्गलधारिणे॥२॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय गौर्यै च ह्यर्धनारीश्वराय च।
उमादेहार्धधारिन्ये नमो भगवते शिव॥३॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय सर्वाय विश्वरूपधराय च।
विश्वनाशाय विश्वाय भूतेशाय नमो नमः॥४॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय रुद्राय मृत्युञ्जयाय शम्भवे।
सर्वज्ञाय सदाशम्भो करुणामयदृष्टये॥५॥

पञ्चरत्नमिदं प्रोक्तं शिवस्य परमात्मनः।
यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय शिवलोकमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्रीशिवपञ्चकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Shiva Panchakshara Stotram ||

Om Namah Shivaaya Shaantaaya Kaaranatrayahetave.
Nivedayaami Chaatmaanam Tvam Gatih Parameshvara.||1||

Om Namah Shivaaya Shuddhaaya Brahmaroopadharaaya Cha.
Sachchidaanandaroopaaya Krishnapingaladhaarine.||2||

Om Namah Shivaaya Gauryai Cha Hyardhanaareshvaraaya Cha.
Umaadehaarddhadharinye Namo Bhagavate Shiva.||3||

Om Namah Shivaaya Sarvaaya Vishvaroopadharaaya Cha.
Vishvanaashaaya Vishvaaya Bhooteshaaya Namo Namah.||4||

Om Namah Shivaaya Rudraaya Mrityunjayaaya Shambhave.
Sarvajnaaya Sadaashambho Karunaamayadrishtaye.||5||

Pancharatnamidam Proktam Shivasya Paramaatmanah.
Yah Pathetpraataruttthaaya Shivalokamavaapnuyaat.

|| Iti Shri Shiva Panchakam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. शान्त, तीन कारणों (सृष्टि-स्थिति-संहार) के हेतु शिव को ॐ नमः शिवाय। हे परमेश्वर! मैं अपने आत्मा को आपको निवेदित करता हूँ, आप ही मेरी गति हैं।

२. शुद्ध, ब्रह्मरूपधारी, सच्चिदानन्दस्वरूप, कृष्णपिङ्गलधारी (अर्धकृष्ण-अर्धपिंगल जटा वाले) शिव को ॐ नमः शिवाय।

३. गौरी सहित, अर्धनारीश्वर, उमा के देह का अर्ध धारण करने वाले भगवान शिव को ॐ नमः शिवाय।

४. सर्वस्वरूप, विश्वरूपधारी, विश्व का नाश करने वाले, विश्वस्वरूप, भूतों के ईश्वर शिव को ॐ नमः शिवाय, बारम्बार नमस्कार।

५. रुद्र, मृत्युञ्जय, शम्भु, सर्वज्ञ, सदाशम्भु, करुणामय दृष्टि वाले शिव को ॐ नमः शिवाय।

परमात्मा शिव का यह पञ्चरत्न (पाँच श्लोक रूपी रत्न) कहा गया है। जो प्रातःकाल उठकर इसका पाठ करता है, वह शिवलोक को प्राप्त करता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीशिवपञ्चकस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य पुराणोक्त ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री शिव देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। शिव कृपा शक्तिः। शिवलोक प्राप्ति कीलकम्। श्रीशिवप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Short and powerful five-verse praise of Lord Shiva
Each verse begins with the sacred Panchakshara mantra Om Namah Shivaya
Invokes shivaloka (abode of shiva) to regular reciters
Purifies the mind and grants inner peace
Surrenders the self completely to Parameshvara
description: The Shiva Panchakam, known in Sanskrit as शिव पञ्चाक्षर स्तोत्रम्, is a set of five devotional verses in praise of Lord Shiva. This sacred composition is sourced from Shiva Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to surrender the self completely to Parameshvara as well as short and powerful five-verse praise of Lord Shiva. Additionally, it is known to purify the mind and grant inner peace. This sacred text is ideally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month for enhanced blessings. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 3 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shiva Panchakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiva Panchakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five verses of devotion to Lord Shiva starting with Om Namah Shivaya.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shiva Panchakam and what does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiva Panchakam (शिव पञ्चकम्) is a short and exceptionally powerful five-verse Sanskrit hymn in praise of Lord Shiva, where each of the five verses opens with the supreme Panchakshara mantra 'Om Namah Shivaya' and addresses Shiva by a different aspect of his nature. The five verses sequentially praise Shiva as: (1) Shanta (the peaceful one) and karana-traya-hetu (cause of the three causes — sristhi, sthiti, samhara); (2) Shuddha (the pure), Brahmarupa-dhara (bearer of the form of Brahman) and Sat-Chid-Ananda-rupa (the embodiment of being-consciousness-bliss); (3) Ardhanarishvara — half united with Devi Gauri/Uma; (4) Sarva, Vishvarupa-dhara, Bhutesha — the all, the cosmic form, the lord of all beings; and (5) Rudra, Mrityunjaya, Shambhu and Sadashambhu — the conqueror of death and the ever-auspicious. The phalashruti promises Shivaloka to those who recite it at dawn. (Sources: classical Shiva stotra tradition; Shiva Purana lineage.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Shiva Panchakam the same as the Shiva Panchakshara Stotram by Adi Shankaracharya?
seoFaqs.answer: The two are closely related but technically distinct. The famous Shiva Panchakshara Stotram by Sri Adi Shankaracharya is the well-known five-verse hymn beginning 'Nagendraharaya Trilochanaya Bhasmangaragaya Maheshvaraya, Nityaya Shuddhaya Digambaraya Tasmai Nakaraya Namah Shivaya' — where each verse builds the five sacred letters Na-Ma-Shi-Va-Ya of the Panchakshara mantra in turn. The Shiva Panchakam on this page is a separate but parallel composition, also five-versed and also opening each verse with Om Namah Shivaya, drawn from the broader Shiva stotra tradition, with verses praising Shanta-rupa, Shuddha-rupa, Ardhanarishvara, Vishvarupa and Mrityunjaya forms. Both are concise daily-recitation hymns and many devotees chant them together. The Shiva Pancharatnam is yet a third related composition. (Sources: Shankara stotra-mala; classical Shiva stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Om Namah Shivaya mean and why does each verse open with it?
seoFaqs.answer: Om Namah Shivaya is the supreme Panchakshara — the five-syllable mantra (na-ma-shi-va-ya) which is the heart-mantra of Lord Shiva, declared in the Shri Rudram of the Yajurveda (Yajurveda 4.5.41) and praised across all Shaiva agamas as the maha-mantra capable alone of bestowing moksha. Word-by-word: Om — pranava, the sacred sound that is Brahman; namah — salutation, surrender, bowing-down; Shivaya — to Shiva, the auspicious one, the supreme reality. By opening each of the five verses with Om Namah Shivaya, the Shiva Panchakam structurally weaves the Panchakshara into every aspect of Shiva it praises — Shanta, Shuddha, Ardhanarishvara, Vishvarupa, Mrityunjaya — making the entire composition a five-fold Panchakshara meditation. Each verse is therefore at once a salutation, a meditation and a complete japa. (Sources: Yajurveda 4.5.41; Shiva Purana; classical Shaiva mantra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the verse Om Namah Shivaya Gauryai Cha Hyardhanarishvaraya?
seoFaqs.answer: The third verse — Om Namah Shivaya Gauryai cha hi Ardhanarishvaraya, Umadeha-ardha-dharinye namo bhagavate Shiva — is the Ardhanarishvara verse of the Shiva Panchakam. It offers salutation simultaneously to Shiva, to Devi Gauri (Parvati), and to the unified form of Ardhanarishvara — 'the Lord who is half woman' — the iconic form in which the right half of the body is Shiva and the left half is Uma/Parvati, joined inseparably as one. The verse worships Shiva as Uma-deha-ardha-dharinye, 'the bearer of half of Uma's body,' affirming the essential Shaiva-Shakta truth that Shiva and Shakti are not two but one, that pure consciousness (Shiva) and dynamic power (Shakti) are inseparable like fire and its heat, and that worship of Shiva is incomplete without worship of his eternal consort. (Sources: Shiva Purana Ardhanarishvara; classical Shaiva-Shakta tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits and phala does the Shiva Panchakam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti (closing benefit-verse) of the Shiva Panchakam states: Pancharatnam-idam proktam Shivasya paramatmanah, yah pathet pratar-utthaya Shivalokam-avapnuyat — 'These five jewels of the Supreme Shiva have been declared; whoever recites them rising at dawn attains the Shiva-loka.' Traditional teachers attribute the following further benefits: complete surrender of the self to Parameshvara through verse one; mental purification and absorption in Sat-Chid-Ananda through verse two; harmonious union of inner Shiva-Shakti and marital harmony through verse three; expansion of consciousness into cosmic awareness through verse four; conquest of fear of death and removal of sins through the Mrityunjaya invocation in verse five; and the supreme phala of Shivaloka — the eternal abode of Lord Shiva — for those who make daily morning recitation a discipline. (Sources: Shiva Panchakam phalashruti; classical Shaiva tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Shiva Panchakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiva Panchakam, being short (only five verses, 3 minutes recitation), is ideally suited for daily morning sadhana. The classical vidhi: (1) Wake before sunrise at Brahma muhurta; (2) Bathe and wear clean clothes (white or rudra-rich colours); (3) Sit facing east or north on a clean asana; (4) Light a ghee lamp before a Shiva linga, picture or yantra; (5) Offer bilva patra, water, white flowers, akshat and bhasma; (6) Recite Om Namah Shivaya 21 or 108 times as preliminary japa; (7) Recite the Shiva Panchakam slowly with concentration on the meaning of each verse, ideally three or five times; (8) Conclude with Shiva Aarti and prasada distribution. Especially powerful on Mondays (Somvar), every Pradosh tithi, Maha Shivaratri (Phalguna Krishna Chaturdashi), the entire Shravan month, Krittika nakshatra and Kartik Purnima. (Sources: classical Shiva nityapaath paddhati.)
